page: The Vultr vcg-a40-2c-10g-4vram is a virtual machine within the Cloud GPU family, built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 shared Intel vCPUs derived from a single physical core with 2 threads, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor is supported by 16 MB of L3 cache and 4 MB of L2 cache. System memory totals 10.0 GB, providing 5.0 GB of RAM per core. For storage, the instance includes 180 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity, and it comes provisioned with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear division between CPU and GPU capabilities. The shared CPU architecture delivers average single-core performance in stress-ng and standard compression tasks like gzip and bzip3. However, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the weak to poor categories. In contrast, the GPU component, which consists of a fractional 0.0833 slice of an NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 4 GB of VRAM, demonstrates strong performance in prompt processing for both small (135M) and medium (7B) language models.

This fractional GPU allocation offers a highly cost-efficient alternative to provisioning full, dedicated GPU hardware. By allowing users to access a small slice of an enterprise-grade NVIDIA Ampere card, the instance lowers the financial barrier for entry-level accelerated tasks. It is best suited for lightweight LLM inference, prompt processing, model testing, and GPU-based software development, whereas it is poorly suited for heavy multi-threaded CPU computations or high-throughput database workloads.
description: The Vultr vcg-a40-2c-10g-4vram is an entry-level Cloud GPU instance featuring 2 shared Intel x86_64 vCPUs, 10.0 GB of RAM, and 180 GB of NVMe SSD storage. It integrates a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU (0.0833 allocation) with 4 GB of VRAM. Benchmark data indicates that while shared CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations are weak, the instance delivers strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium language models. This resource profile provides a cost-efficient entry point for developers requiring GPU acceleration without the overhead of a dedicated card. It is highly suited for lightweight LLM inference, model prototyping, and GPU application testing.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a40-2c-10g-4vram is a Cloud GPU instance featuring 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 10 GB RAM, 180 GB NVMe SSD, and a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 4 GB VRAM for entry-level accelerated workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a40-2c-10g-4vram is a Cloud GPU instance with 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 10 GB RAM, and a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 4 GB VRAM.
tagline: A budget-friendly fractional GPU instance combining shared Intel processors with NVIDIA Ampere acceleration for lightweight model inference and development.
bullet_points: Fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 4 GB VRAM
2 shared Intel x86_64 vCPUs with 10.0 GB RAM
180 GB local NVMe SSD storage included
Strong prompt processing for small and medium LLMs
Cost-efficient profile for entry-level GPU development
